content:
❌ Rejected the contribution.
Comment: The validators are the same as the test cases, this would allow hard-coded solutions. If you change the validators, I would approve it.
Btw, I think this could make a great reverse puzzle because the symmetry of the diamond can be a pattern to look for. I really advocate for reverse

Goal
* Please rewrite as follows for clarity:

Given an integer [[n]], output a symmetric diamond-shaped digit pattern. For a diamond of size [[n]], the rules are:

• Each row [[i]] ([[i]] = 1 to [[n]]) starts from the integer [[i]].

• Digits in each row increase step by step toward the center and then mirror back.

• The middle row is the widest row containing (2 * [[n]] - 1) digits with no spaces. All other rows are centered by adding spaces on the left.

• The bottom half of the diamond is a vertically symmetric copy of the top half (excluding the middle row).

Input
* Please format "n" as a [[variable]].

Output
* Please revise as follows (the rules need not be repeated here):

<<1 or more lines>>: The diamond pattern of digits of size [[n]].

Constraints
* Please format "n" as a [[variable]].

Cases
* Not an issue, but since (almost) all possible values are already used in both tests and validators, it's unnecessary to shuffle the order. The tests remain identical to the validators even if their order are different.

* [[n]] = 4 in both Validators 1 and 5. Please revise the value of [[n]] to 3 in Validator 1.

Thanks.

i Guess i should make this Reverse mode so it became more challenging instead of explaining to much in the question ?
Thanks for review as this is my first contribution
i think about that and i believe its a good idea to make it more challenging

To @element73: I agree with @Hyperform99. Please change your Validators so that they are different from your test cases. The only Validator you shouldn't change is the one where [[n]] = 1 because I feel that it is an edge case. If you feel you are tight on options, I suggest also increasing the upper boundary of your constraint to something like 10. Thanks a lot!

I have updated the validators for all test cases except n = 1 . Please let me know if you have any further suggestions.
Thank you.

Honestly, there's no practical way to make the tests different from the validators in this contribution. The most the author can do (and has done) is vary the order, but since the only valid values of [[n]] are 1 through 5, the tests and validators will inevitably end up being identical.
With VINCE_MX's upper boundary change, the validators could be made different.
You need corresponding rule changes as well, because it isn't clearer what digits to use above 9.

Honestly, the challenge in Reverse mode is somewhat reduced because all test cases are visible (validators = tests).

You may also choose to enable one or more modes.

And of course, you can enable Reverse mode only and remove all explanations.

Goal
* Change "2*[[n]]-1" to "2 × [[n]] − 1" for consistency.

Thanks.
i have done all suggested changes but i don't know why no one is Upvoting or Approving what can be done about it ?
You need patience. Upvotes and approvals can happen quite randomly, and activity usually slows down during the weekend.
